text:
There was a bird named Bill. Bill lived in a cozy little nest near a babbling brook. Spring blossomed, and the birds gathered honeydewberries from wild bees. One day, Bill decided he'd enjoy a swim across the brook. He took flight first. Bill was an adventurous little guy.

As he floated across, he noticed something odd at the edge. There was a patch of moss where a butterfly rested. Feeling unusually empathetic, Bill decided it deserved a bath. Returning to land, he meticulously cleaned himself. He even took a moment to appreciate the butterfly.

On his way back, he noticed a tiny droplet of water trickling down his wing. His curiosity piqued. He scooped it up and looked closer. It became transparent, then gleamed like starlight.

Bill felt a warmth deep inside him. It wasn't just an instinct. It was magic. The droplet glowed softly, and Bill discovered he could understand the forgotten songs of
